label: Model-based reinforcement learning

node.label: Model-based reinforcement learning
node.definition: The agent learns or uses a predictive environment model to evaluate and plan future actions.
node.why_it_matters: In this atlas graph, Model-based reinforcement learning is modeled as depending on Reinforcement learning, Latent variables & hidden state and feeding downstream nodes such as Latent world models & predictive dynamics, World-model rollout & imagination, Game-playing, control & simulation agents.
examples: atlas downstream link to Latent world models & predictive dynamics
atlas downstream link to World-model rollout & imagination
atlas downstream link to Game-playing, control & simulation agents
limitations: The chosen learning signal and interaction pattern may not match the deployment objective or data-generating process. Its direct dependencies include Reinforcement learning, Latent variables & hidden state.

dependencies.rationale: Model-based reinforcement learning follows the learning setup expressed by Reinforcement learning.

In this atlas graph, Model-based reinforcement learning has a `uses` dependency on Latent variables & hidden state because Latent variables & hidden state is modeled as a reusable ingredient for the node.

In this atlas graph, Model-based reinforcement learning has a `uses` dependency on Multimodal, simulated & streaming data because Multimodal, simulated & streaming data is modeled as a reusable ingredient for the node.

In this atlas graph, Model-based reinforcement learning has a `uses` dependency on Dynamical systems & control theory because Dynamical systems & control theory is modeled as a reusable ingredient for the node.
